2021 in Legacy Fighting Alliance
Information
PromotionLegacy Fighting Alliance
First date airedJanuary 15, 2021 (2021-01-15)
Last date airedDecember 10, 2021 (2021-12-10)
← 2020
2022 →

2021 was the fifth year in the history of Legacy Fighting Alliance, a mixed martial arts promotion based in the United States.[1]

LFA Middleweight Tournament

[edit]

In 2021, LFA organized a Middleweight tournament that will crown the Middleweight champion. [2]

Legacy Fighting Alliance 97: Browne vs. Estrázulas

[edit]
Legacy Fighting Alliance 97: Browne vs. Estrázulas
PromotionLegacy Fighting Alliance
DateJanuary 15, 2021
VenueHartman Arena
CityPark City, Kansas, U.S.
Event chronology
Legacy Fighting Alliance 96: Mendonça vs. Dagvadorj Legacy Fighting Alliance 97: Browne vs. Estrázulas Legacy Fighting Alliance 98: Fremd vs. Oliveira

Legacy Fighting Alliance 97: Browne vs. Estrázulas is the ninety-eighth event of Legacy Fighting Alliance and will take place on January 15, 2021. It aired on UFC Fight Pass.[3]

Background

[edit]

The event was headlined by a fight between Nick Browne and Arthur Estrazulas, for the vacant LFA Lightweight title.[4]

In the co-main event, the former LFA Lightweight title challenger Jacob Rosales faced Jose Martinez.[5]

Results

[edit]
Main Card
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Lightweight Nick Browne def. Arthur Estrázulas Decision (unanimous) 5 5:00 [a]
Lightweight Jacob Rosales def. Jose Martinez Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00
Featherweight Kamuela Kirk def. Guilherme Santos TKO (punches) 1 4:25
Women's Strawweight Loveth Young def. Kelly D'Angelo TKO (punches) 2 1:36
Featherweight Michael Stack def. Jordan Mapa TKO (punches) 2 4:00
Welterweight Josh Quinlan def. Joe Boerschig TKO (punches) 3 3:28
Heavyweight Thomas Petersen def. Kimo Luis KO (punch) 1 0:19
Preliminary Card
Women's Flyweight Claire Guthrie def. Nadine Mandiau Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00
Lightweight Shane Shapiro def. Cam Ansel Submission (kimura) 1 1:44
  1. ^ For the vacant LFA Lightweight Championship.

[edit]

The event was headlined by a middleweight bout between Josh Fremd and Bruno Oliveira, which serves as the first middleweight tournament semi-final. [7]

Legacy Fighting Alliance 100: Altamirano vs. Smith

[edit]

Event chronology

Legacy Fighting Alliance 100: Altamirano vs. Smith is the hundredth-first event of Legacy Fighting Alliance and took place on February 19, 2021. It aired on UFC Fight Pass.

Background

[edit]

The event was supposed to be headlined by a flyweight bout between LFA's all-time wins leader Victor "El Magnifico" Altamirano and top undefeated Brazilian prospect Carlos "Tizil" Mota. The co-main event will showcase the second middleweight tournament semi-final between LFA standouts Anthony "SugaFoot" Adams and Gregory "RoboCop" Rodrigues.[9] However, on February 13, Carlos Mota was pulled from the fight and replaced by Nate Smith. [10]

Results

[edit]
Main Card
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Flyweight Victor Altamirano def. Nate Smith Decision (unanimous) 5 5:00 [a]
Flyweight Charles Johnson def. Karlee Pangilinan Submission (anaconda choke) 2 1:41
Featherweight Steven Nguyen def. Jorge Juarez KO (punches) 1 0:30
Lightweight Tommy Aaron def. Gerald Scott Submission (rear-naked choke) 3 3:57
Bantamweight Christian Natividad def. Evan Woolsey Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00
Preliminary Card
Women's Bantamweight Emmanuela Moliere def. Amber Danielson-Smith Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00
Amateur Bouts
Women's Strawweight Maria Henderson def. Amanda Mitchell Decision (unanimous) 3 3:00
  1. ^ For the vacant LFA Flyweight Championship.

The main event featured a featherweight showdown between LFA standouts Bruno "The Tiger" Souza vs. Elijah "Baby" Johns. The co-main event showcased the second middleweight tournament semi-final bout between Dana White's Contender Series alumni Gregory Rodrigues and Al "Sweetness" Matavao.[12]

Legacy Fighting Alliance 107: Kirk vs. Swain

[edit]

Legacy Fighting Alliance 106: Silveira vs. Viana Legacy Fighting Alliance 107: Kirk vs. Swain Legacy Fighting Alliance 108: Fremd vs. Rodrigues

Legacy Fighting Alliance 107: Kirk vs. Swain is the hundredth-eighth event of Legacy Fighting Alliance and took place on May 14, 2021. It aired on UFC Fight Pass.

Background

[edit]

The main event featured a middleweight showdown between two of the most accomplished fighters in the division. Rafael Carvalho, former Bellator Middleweight Champion, faced emerging Tajik, Sharaf Davlatmurodov, who was riding a 5-fight win streak, which includes a win over Bellator middleweight tournament runner-up Brett Cooper. [17] The day after the announcement of the bout, it was announced that Carvalho was to face Lorenz Larkin at Bellator 258.[18]

Results

[edit]
Main Card
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Featherweight Kamuela Kirk def. Daniel Swain TKO (retirement) 2 5:00
Bantamweight Ricardo Dias def. George Garcia KO (knee) 2 0:25
Bantamweight Allan Begosso def. Samarbek Sabyrzhan Uulu Submission (guillotine choke) 1 4:23
Heavyweight Jordan Heiderman def. Anthony Garrett Submission (arm-triangle choke) 1 3:40
Lightweight James Wilson def. Daniel Jefferson Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00
Women's Flyweight Taylor Mauldin def. Ambar De La Herran Submission (kneebar) 1 1:30
Preliminary Card
Welterweight Ray Waters def. L.J. Portée Submission (rear-naked choke) 1 1:02
Welterweight Corey Davis def. Thomas Powell TKO (head kick) 2 2:50
Women's Strawweight Alandria Brown def. Shania Arredondo Submission (rear-naked choke) 3 3:54

Legacy Fighting Alliance 108: Fremd vs. Rodrigues

Legacy Fighting Alliance 108: Fremd vs. Rodrigues is the hundredth-ninth event of Legacy Fighting Alliance and took place on May 21, 2021. It aired on UFC Fight Pass.

Background

[edit]

The main event featured a middleweight title fight between two of the most exciting knockout artists in the division. Josh Fremd (7-1) and Gregory Rodrigues (8-3) both earned their shot at the LFA Middleweight Championship by advancing to the finale of the LFA Middleweight Tournament, which began in January. [17]

At weigh-ins, Clayton Carpenter came in at 128.6 pounds, 2.6 pounds over the non-championship flyweight limit. Therefore, the fight continued at catchweight and he was fined a percentage of his purse.[19]

Results

[edit]
Main Card
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Middleweight Gregory Rodrigues def. Josh Fremd KO (punches) 1 2:20 [a]
Flyweight Mark Climaco def. Rodney Kealohi Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00
Welterweight Tyler Ray def. Ernesto Ancona Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00
Women's Strawweight Kathryn Paprocki def. Tiani Valle Technical Submission (rear-naked choke) 3 4:01
Catchweight (128.6 lbs) Clayton Carpenter def. Nick Clem Submission (kneebar) 1 0:45
Bantamweight Bryce Meredith def. Steven Merrill TKO (punches) 1 3:35
Preliminary Card
Welterweight Kenan Jackson def. Blayne Richards Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00
Heavyweight Bailey Schoenfelder def. James Munoz TKO (punches) 1 0:20
Welterweight Archie Colgan def. Keith Phathaem Submission (arm-triangle choke) 1 4:53
Amateur Bouts
Middleweight Jeff Nielsen def. C.J. Hays TKO (punches) 2 2:39
Women's Bantamweight Eva Brundage def. Cheyanne Bowers Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00
Catchweight (140 lbs) Colten Larson def. Thomas Vievering TKO (punches) 2 1:23
  1. ^ For the vacant LFA Middleweight Championship.

Terrance McKinney, coming off a knockout win at LFA 107, is looking to solidify himself at the top of the lightweight division. He will face top Puerto Rican prospect Michael Irizarry in the main event of LFA 109.[20]

Legacy Fighting Alliance 110: Johnson vs. Horiuchi is the hundredth-eleventh event of Legacy Fighting Alliance and took place on July 2, 2021. It aired on UFC Fight Pass.

Background

[edit]

The main event will feature a LFA Light Heavyweight Championship title fight between top undefeated prospect Joshua Silveira and regional kingpin Jesse "Hollywood" Murray. The co-main event will also feature a title fight when top contenders Charles "Inner G" Johnson and Yuma Horiuchi vie for interim LFA Flyweight Championship.[21] On June 29, Silveira vs. Murray was postponed to a later date and the Flyweight Interim title bout was promoted to the main event.[22]

Results

[edit]
Main Card
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Flyweight Charles Johnson def. Yuma Horiuchi Decision (split) 5 5:00 [a]
Middleweight Hemerson Souza def. Chibwikem Onyenegecha Decision (split) 3 5:00
Flyweight Christian Natividad def. Luis Aguirre Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00
Women's Strawweight Jaqueline Amorim def. Megan Owen KO (punches) 1 0:10
Flyweight Oscar Herrera def. Arpan Gurung Submission (armbar) 2 3:17
  1. ^ For the interim LFA Flyweight Championship.

Legacy Fighting Alliance 112: Welterweight Tournament is the hundredth-thirteenth event of Legacy Fighting Alliance and took place on July 19, 2021. It aired on UFC Fight Pass.

Background

[edit]

The event featured a welterweight tournament, held to determine the new LFA champion. Gabriel Bonfim met Brenner Alberth in the first semifinal bout, while Diego Dias met Carlos Leal in the second semifinal bout.[23]

[edit]
